name: Sleep Preparation
description: A gentle session designed to help you drift into deep, restful sleep using autogenic training, progressive relaxation, and soothing cloud imagery.

segments.text: Lie down in your bed and allow yourself to settle into a comfortable position. You don't need to do anything or go anywhere. The only thing left on your agenda tonight is sleep.

Gently close your eyes. Take a slow breath in through your nose, and let it out softly through your mouth. With that exhale, let go of the day — every thought, every task, every worry. They will still be there tomorrow. Right now, sleep is the only thing that matters.
Notice the weight of your body against the mattress. Feel the pillow supporting your head. The sheets feel just right. Everything around you is quiet and calm.
This moment is yours. There is no hurry. You are safe, comfortable, and free to drift into deep, healing sleep.
We will begin with autogenic training — a gentle practice of giving your body simple, soothing instructions. Allow each phrase to sink in at your own pace, without effort.
My right arm is heavy. My right arm is heavy and warm. Feel the pleasant weight spreading through your arm, from shoulder to fingertips.
My left arm is heavy. My left arm is heavy and warm. Both arms feel pleasantly heavy, sinking deeper into the mattress.
My right leg is heavy. My right leg is heavy and warm. My left leg is heavy. My left leg is heavy and warm. Your legs feel wonderfully weighted and relaxed.
My arms and legs are heavy and warm. My whole body is beginning to feel delightfully heavy — not uncomfortable, but like a warm, gentle weight settling you deeper and deeper into the bed.
My heartbeat is calm and regular. My heartbeat is calm and regular. Simply notice the steady, reliable rhythm of your heart — beating gently, keeping you safe, inviting you toward sleep.
My breathing is slow and easy. My breathing is slow and easy. Each breath comes and goes effortlessly, like gentle waves lapping at a quiet shore.
My solar plexus is warm. Deep in your abdomen, there is a warm, glowing feeling — like a gentle warmth spreading outward through your entire body.
My forehead is cool and clear. Your mind grows calm and still. Thoughts slow down, becoming soft and distant, like sounds heard from far away.
Now let a wave of deep relaxation begin at the very top of your head. Feel your scalp soften. Your forehead smooths. Your eyelids feel wonderfully heavy, as though weighted with sleep.
The relaxation flows into your face — your cheeks, your jaw. Let your jaw hang slightly loose. Your mouth may open just a little. That's perfectly fine.
Down through your neck and shoulders — feel any last traces of tension simply dissolve. Your shoulders sink back and down. Your neck feels long and comfortable.
The wave continues through your chest and upper back. Feel your chest rise and fall with each slow breath. Your back is fully supported, completely at ease.
Down through your abdomen, lower back, and hips. Feel yourself sinking ever deeper into the mattress — every muscle releasing, every cell resting.
Down through your thighs, knees, calves. Your legs feel wonderfully heavy and warm. Ankles, feet, toes — all releasing, all at rest.
Your whole body is now deeply, completely relaxed. You are drifting into a place just between waking and sleep — that beautiful, soft boundary where rest begins.

I will count down from ten to one. With each number, you drift a little deeper, a little closer to the edge of sleep. Ten… nine… drifting… eight… seven… so comfortable… six… five… almost there… four… three… so sleepy… two… one. Deep, beautiful rest.
Imagine you are lying in a soft, warm cloud. This cloud holds you perfectly — like the gentlest hammock in the world. The cloud is exactly the right temperature. Everything is perfectly still.
Your cloud drifts slowly through a dark, peaceful sky. Below, the world carries on — but it is very far away and very quiet. Up here, there is only peace and darkness and rest.
Allow these words to settle softly into your mind, like snowflakes settling on still water: I drift into deep, restful sleep. Sleep comes easily and naturally to me.
My mind quiets naturally when I lie down. I release the day with ease. I trust my body to rest and restore itself through the night.
I sleep deeply and peacefully through the night. I wake feeling fully rested, refreshed, and renewed. Sleep is a safe and natural state for me.
Your cloud drifts on. The sky is dark and quiet. Your breathing slows even further. Your thoughts grow softer, vaguer, like half-remembered dreams.
There is nothing to do, nothing to think about, nothing to solve. Simply rest. Simply let go. Simply drift. Sleep is coming to meet you now.
This session is now complete. There is no need to rouse yourself fully — you are free to drift directly into sleep from this comfortable, relaxed state.
Simply let your awareness fade gently, like a candle flame slowly dimming. Your body knows what to do next. Sleep is natural. Sleep is safe. Sleep is here.
Rest well. Good night.
